entries.title: Two of the most recognizable people on the planet—President Trump and Pope Leo XIV—appear to be going at it…on social media, of all places. Read more in today’s WSJ politics newsletter:
President Trump lashed out at Pope Leo XIV following the pontiff’s public condemnation of the war in Iran, accusing the leader of the Catholic Church of being weak on crime and catering to liberals
President Trump and his advisers have been considering the Wall Street and Main Street impact of continued fighting in the Middle East
Rep. Eric Swalwell said he was suspending his campaign for California governor, following allegations of sexual misconduct that sparked outrage among fellow Democrats
The Internal Revenue Service shed thousands of enforcement agents—tempting more Americans to cheat on their taxes.
Jeanine Pirro’s ability to manage a mercurial president has helped make her a dark-horse candidate in MAGA circles to become the next U.S. attorney general
Before taking office, RFK Jr. promised to end what he dubbed the FDA’s “aggressive suppression” of raw milk. Now leading the HHS, he has pivoted to the politically safer choice of whole milk.
We visited influencer Jessica Reed Kraus’s mocktail night with her MAHA friends to talk about voting, policy and RFK Jr.’s tenure
MAHA activists visit the White House as Republicans try to win back a key voting bloc ahead of the midterm elections
President Trump has repeatedly promised his top administration officials pardons before he leaves office, according to people who have heard his comments
Maryland Gov. Wes Moore said the U.S. is risking a protracted conflict in Iran similar to the ones in Iraq and Afghanistan
Powerful Democrats are calling for Rep. Eric Swalwell to end his campaign for California governor, citing allegations of sexual misconduct. Swalwell called the allegations “flat false.”
Republican Clayton Fuller won the special election for Georgia’s 14th Congressional District, formerly held by Marjorie Taylor Greene
Retired Army Lt. Col. Alexander Vindman is a Democrat running for Senate in solidly Republican Florida
President Trump released mock-up images of a gleaming glass tower emblazoned with his last name that will serve as his presidential library once he leaves office
Missouri Republican Rep. Sam Graves, chairman of the House Committee on Transportation, is pulling his plans to seek re-election
Pope Leo Criticizes Religious Language Used by Trump on the War
The Defense Department violated a court order to restore Pentagon access for journalists, a federal judge ruled on Thursday, a setback for the Trump administration
President Trump’s declaration of “total victory” in Iran left some close allies and several senior aides worried that he’s overstating what is a fragile cease-fire with Tehran
A federal appeals court has denied Anthropic’s request for relief from the Defense Department declaring it a supply-chain risk, complicating the legal battle between the government and the AI company
For nearly two days, injured and alone, a U.S. aviator hid in a remote mountain crevice as Iranian forces and militias closed in on him
